title: US-Iran Negotiations and the Case for Crude Tanker Capacity
section_path: US-Iran Negotiations and the Case for Crude Tanker Capacity
section_path_text: US-Iran Negotiations and the Case for Crude Tanker Capacity
summary:
Main
As US-Iran nuclear negotiations move through their latest round, the shipping market is watching closely. A credible deal that reopens Iranian crude exports would add significant volume to global flows, but the route and insurance landscape would take time to normalise, and Hormuz-related risk is not about to disappear overnight. Against that backdrop, Allied QuantumSea Research has identified a clear new trend in newbuilding activity: owners are not waiting.
